page.content: The first table presents data on various populations with their corresponding strains, CDEII S, CDEII π, TE S, TE π, P values, and CDEII:TE ratios. The Global population has 34 strains, a CDEII S of 222, a CDEII π of 0.04, a TE S of 1715, a TE π of 0.01, a P value of 9 × 10-11, and a CDEII:TE ratio of 3.6. The European population consists of 11 strains, with a CDEII S of 29, a CDEII π of 0.006, a TE S of 133, a TE π of 0.001, a P value of 2 × 10-10, and a CDEII:TE ratio of 4.8. The Sake population has 3 strains, a CDEII S of 27, a CDEII π of 0.01, a TE S of 32, a TE π of 0.005, a P value of 6 × 10-6, and a CDEII:TE ratio of 2.6. The Malaysian population features 3 strains, a CDEII S of 1, a CDEII π of 0.0005, a TE S of 0, a TE π of 0, a P value of 0.02, and an n/a CDEII:TE ratio. The Oak population has 2 strains with all CDEII and TE metrics at 0 and an n/a CDEII:TE ratio. The West African population also has 2 strains with similar metrics and an n/a CDEII:TE ratio. The second table outlines diseases and their associated allergens, listing Burning mouth syndrome linked to Potassium dicyanoaurate, Lichenois reactions also linked to Potassium dicyanoaurate, Cheilitis associated with Aroma mixtures, Stomatitis connected to Mercury, Gingivitis related to Potassium dicyanoaurate, Orofacial granulomatosis linked to Nickel sulfatehexahydrate, Perioral dermatitis associated with Cobalt–chloride, and Recurrent stomatitis aphtosa related to Vanillin.
